title: pseudotsuga taxifolia var. brevibracteata
intro: Pseudotsuga taxifolia var. brevibracteata, also known as the mountain pine or short-bracted Douglas fir, is a lesser-known variety of the Douglas fir tree, characterized by its smaller, scale-like leaves and short-bracted cones. The bark of this tree has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and improving digestion. In herbal preparations, the bark is often dried and made into teas or infused in hot water to extract its medicinal properties, though more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
